seo_title: Current Time in San Salvador — CST (UTC-6)
seo_description: Current time in San Salvador, El Salvador. Live CST clock — UTC-6 year-round, no Daylight Saving Time. El Salvador time vs New York, Los Angeles, Miami, Houston.

faq.question: What time is it in San Salvador right now?
faq.answer: San Salvador, El Salvador uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) year-round. El Salvador does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/El_Salvador. San Salvador operates on Central Standard Time (CST), which is UTC-6 year-round. El Salvador does not observe Daylight Saving Time, so the clock never changes. Use the live clock above to see the exact current time in San Salvador with live seconds.

faq.question: What time zone is San Salvador in?
faq.answer: San Salvador is in the Central Standard Time zone (CST), fixed at UTC-6 throughout the entire year. El Salvador shares this permanent UTC-6 offset with Guatemala and most of Central America (Honduras, Nicaragua, and Costa Rica also use UTC-6 without DST). San Salvador is 1 hour behind New York during Eastern Standard Time in winter, and 2 hours behind when New York switches to Eastern Daylight Time in summer.

faq.question: Does San Salvador observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: No. El Salvador does not observe Daylight Saving Time and has not done so since 1987. Clocks remain permanently at UTC-6. This means El Salvador's offset from the US changes seasonally: it is 1 hour behind New York in winter (EST) and 2 hours behind in summer (EDT), because the US shifts but El Salvador does not.

faq.question: What is the time difference between San Salvador and New York?
faq.answer: San Salvador is 1 hour behind New York during Eastern Standard Time (EST/UTC-5) in winter, and 2 hours behind during Eastern Daylight Time (EDT/UTC-4) in summer. When it is 12:00 PM in New York in January, it is 11:00 AM in San Salvador. In summer, a 12:00 PM New York meeting corresponds to 10:00 AM in San Salvador — the gap doubles because the US observes DST and El Salvador does not.

faq.question: What is the time difference between San Salvador and Los Angeles?
faq.answer: San Salvador is 2 hours ahead of Los Angeles during Pacific Standard Time (PST/UTC-8) in winter, and 1 hour ahead during Pacific Daylight Time (PDT/UTC-7) in summer. When it is 9:00 AM in Los Angeles in January, it is 11:00 AM in San Salvador. The gap shifts seasonally because Los Angeles observes DST and El Salvador does not.

faq.question: What is the time difference between San Salvador and London?
faq.answer: San Salvador is 6 hours behind London during winter (GMT/UTC+0) and 7 hours behind during summer (BST/UTC+1). When it is 3:00 PM in London in December, it is 9:00 AM in San Salvador. The gap widens in summer because London moves to BST while San Salvador stays at UTC-6.

faq.question: What is the best time to call San Salvador from the US East Coast?
faq.answer: From the US East Coast (EST), San Salvador is just 1 hour behind in winter, so any standard business-hours call works easily. Call between 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM Eastern to reach San Salvador's 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST workday. In summer, the gap widens to 2 hours, so callers should adjust: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM Eastern covers San Salvador's business hours of 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST.

faq.question: What are business hours in San Salvador?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in San Salvador run 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM or 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST (UTC-6), Monday through Friday. Government offices typically operate 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM. Retail centres such as Multiplaza and Metrocentro are open 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM or 9:00 PM. El Salvador adopted the US dollar as its official currency in 2001, and in 2021 became the first country to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender — factors that have attracted international fintech and tech investment to the capital.

content_blocks.title: San Salvador Time Zone — CST UTC-6, No Daylight Saving
content_blocks.content: San Salvador, the capital of El Salvador and the country's largest city, operates permanently on Central Standard Time (CST/UTC-6). El Salvador abolished Daylight Saving Time in 1987 and has not observed it since, placing the country on a fixed UTC-6 offset year-round. This aligns San Salvador with Guatemala City, Tegucigalpa (Honduras), and Managua (Nicaragua) — all running on permanent UTC-6. The key practical implication: San Salvador is 1 hour behind New York in winter (EST), but 2 hours behind in summer (EDT), since the US observes DST and El Salvador does not. From Los Angeles, San Salvador is 2 hours ahead in winter and 1 hour ahead in summer. The no-DST policy simplifies scheduling for businesses with consistent regional operations across Central America.

content_blocks.title: El Salvador — Bitcoin Hub, Surf Culture, and Growing Tech Scene
content_blocks.content: San Salvador is the economic and political heart of El Salvador, one of Central America's most densely populated countries. In September 2021, El Salvador made global headlines by becoming the first sovereign nation to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender alongside the US dollar (adopted in 2001). This has attracted cryptocurrency companies, fintech startups, and blockchain developers to establish operations in San Salvador. The government created 'Bitcoin City' at the base of the Conchagua volcano as a planned tax-free crypto zone. Beyond digital finance, El Salvador is renowned as a premier surf destination — El Zonte ('Bitcoin Beach') and La Libertad's Punta Roca offer world-class waves within an hour of the capital. San Salvador's tech and business scene is growing, with coworking spaces, startup accelerators, and a young, digitally connected workforce.

content_blocks.title: Scheduling Calls and Meetings with San Salvador
content_blocks.content: Coordinating with San Salvador from major international business hubs is straightforward, with its UTC-6 fixed offset providing predictability. From New York (EST/UTC-5), the 1-hour gap in winter makes alignment easy — a 10:00 AM New York call reaches San Salvador at 9:00 AM. In summer, the gap widens to 2 hours (EDT vs CST), so 11:00 AM Eastern corresponds to 9:00 AM local. From Miami or Houston (also UTC-6 in CST), there is zero difference in winter; in summer Houston is on CDT (UTC-5) and San Salvador remains at UTC-6, placing San Salvador 1 hour behind. From London, San Salvador is 6–7 hours behind — an afternoon London meeting at 3:00 PM (GMT) aligns with 9:00 AM in San Salvador. From Madrid (CET/UTC+1), the gap is 7 hours in winter. El Salvador's country code is +503; San Salvador uses the city code 2.

time_difference_table.difference: 1 hour behind New York (2 hours behind when NY is on EDT)
6 hours behind London (7 hours behind when London is on BST)
2 hours ahead of Los Angeles (1 hour ahead when LA is on PDT)
1 hour behind Miami (2 hours behind when Miami is on EDT)
Same time as Chicago in winter (1 hour behind when Chicago is on CDT)
1 hour behind Toronto (2 hours behind when Toronto is on EDT)
7 hours behind Madrid (8 hours behind when Madrid is on CEST)
